<a name="Context"></a>
## type Context

Context provides a way for tasks to check if they have been aborted.

```go
type Context struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Context.IsAborted"></a>
### func \(\*Context\) IsAborted

```go
func (c *Context) IsAborted() bool
```

IsAborted returns true if the task has been aborted.

<a name="Task"></a>
## type Task

Task represents a running exclusive task.

```go
type Task[T any] struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="RunExclusively"></a>
### func RunExclusively

```go
func RunExclusively[T any](id string, taskFunc func() T) (*Task[T], error)
```

RunExclusively runs a task function exclusively by ID. If a task with the same ID is already running, it returns an error. The returned Task provides a Done\(\) channel to receive the result and an Abort\(\) method to cancel the task.

<a name="RunExclusivelyWithContext"></a>
### func RunExclusivelyWithContext

```go
func RunExclusivelyWithContext[T any](id string, taskFunc func(*Context) T) (*Task[T], error)
```

RunExclusivelyWithContext runs a task function exclusively by ID, providing a Context that the task can use to check for abort signals.

<a name="Task[T].Abort"></a>
### func \(\*Task\[T\]\) Abort

```go
func (t *Task[T]) Abort()
```

Abort signals the task to stop and removes it from the running tasks. The task function should check Context.IsAborted\(\) to respond to abort requests. After Abort is called, the Done channel will receive the zero value of T.

<a name="Task[T].Done"></a>
### func \(\*Task\[T\]\) Done

```go
func (t *Task[T]) Done() <-chan T
```

Done returns a channel that will receive the result when the task completes.
